questions.prompt: A missing colon at the end of a def statement is what type of error?
questions.explanation: A missing colon is a syntax error. Python catches these before the program runs.

questions.prompt: In a try/except/else block, when does the else block run?
questions.explanation: The else block runs only when the try block completes without any exception.

questions.prompt: Which is the correct way to define a custom exception?
questions.explanation: A custom exception must inherit from Exception: class MyError(Exception): pass

questions.prompt: What does the raise keyword do?
questions.explanation: The raise keyword throws (raises) an exception deliberately when a rule is broken, like insufficient balance.

questions.prompt: When does the finally block execute?
choices: Only when there is no exception
Only when there is an exception
Always, whether or not an exception occurs
questions.explanation: The finally block always executes, regardless of whether an exception occurred. It is used for cleanup tasks.
